planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Assess the stability and generality of the dominant single destination port pattern in DDoS traffic and its impact on model robustness.
key_checks: Measure distributional stability of destination port across temporal and attack scenario subsets
Evaluate entropy and cardinality of destination port within DDoS class over multiple partitions
Check for shortcut-like predictive leverage of destination port alone
Test sensitivity of classification outcomes to perturbations or masking of destination port feature
success_criteria: Evidence that destination port dominance is consistent across diverse temporal and scenario slices
Identification of potential overfitting risk if port usage is narrowly concentrated
Detection of vulnerability to evasion if port distribution shifts
Clear characterization of destination port as a representation-sensitive but brittle indicator

planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Characterize the stability and origin of strong linear dependencies between feature pairs to understand redundancy and potential bias.
key_checks: Analyze correlation stability of identified feature pairs across temporal segments
Compare dependency profiles before and after preprocessing steps
Assess whether dependencies align with known protocol relationships or data transformations
Evaluate impact of dependencies on effective feature dimensionality and model bias potential
success_criteria: Confirmation of stable or unstable dependency patterns over time
Clarification of whether dependencies stem from inherent network behavior or preprocessing
Identification of redundant features that may bias learning
Evidence supporting or weakening the hypothesis of structural redundancy
router_constraints: preserve feature_scope for all involved feature pairs

planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Determine if low diversity in bulk traffic features reflects genuine network behavior or dataset artifact and its effect on discriminative capacity.
key_checks: Quantify cardinality and near-constant behavior of bulk features across dataset partitions
Investigate temporal consistency of low diversity patterns
Explore subtle variations within near-constant features for potential signal
Assess whether low diversity limits or masks relevant attack-related variations
success_criteria: Evidence distinguishing genuine network saturation from feature extraction artifacts
Identification of any subtle but meaningful variations within low diversity features
Understanding of how low diversity affects feature discriminative power
Clarification of whether these features contribute stable or unstable signals
router_constraints: preserve feature_scope for all bulk-related features
